title: The Decision
timelinePosition: One day after Chapter 18. Opens in a field camp south of Stratholme and closes with the formation turned north toward the coast.
openLocation: Farstrider field camp south of Stratholme.
transport: Static camp work -> mounted arrival and departure beats -> sustained road ride north.
immediateObjective: Decide whether the investigation now belongs on the road north, then survive the institutional attempts to pull both women back before that decision is spoken aloud.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Sylvanas's perception only.
No interior access to Jaina's sending-stone call; the scene reads through posture, light, and timing only.
Do not overstate thaw or rapport; the chapter proves chosen alignment through action and severance.

povKnowsAtOpen: Stratholme welded the Scholomance crack shut in practice.
Jaina's revised model now points past contaminated grain toward a source or relay farther north.
Arthas is sailing for Northrend with men already selected by their obedience at Stratholme.
Uther rides west to warn Terenas.
South was only the road out from the wall; it is not where the next real answer lives.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: Whether Jaina will cut the Dalaran tether as cleanly as Sylvanas suspects she must.
How far the Convocation will go to drag Sylvanas home.
Whether Lor'themar still has anything left to spend politically after holding the line this long.
How the formation reaches Northrend with two horses, one escort band, and no institutional cover once the coast is behind them.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No Frostmourne sighting or direct blade identification yet.
No Sylvanas access to Jaina's exact words on the sending-stone.
No suggestion that north is safe just because it is correct.
No softening of desertion, family cost, or institutional severance.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Sylvanas refuses the Convocation's recall and says aloud that the threat to Quel'Thalas is wherever Arthas goes next.
Lor'themar bought a small forward mobilization at immense personal and political cost.
Jaina informs Antonidas she is going north and closes the Dalaran line herself.
The relay network dies at the coast; everything beyond that is competence, borrowed passage, and whatever Jaina can track through resonance.
The chapter ends with the formation riding north and the investigation fully becoming a pursuit.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: The evidence already points north; the only remaining question is what she is willing to lose by obeying it.
pov.surfaceObjective: Turn the post-Stratholme picture into a real direction before the institutions reclaim the people holding it.
pov.pressureSource: Stratholme's smoke behind her, Jaina's north-pointing model beside her, the Convocation waiting at her back, and the knowledge that she aimed Arthas at this crisis in the first place.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: The last pieces of the old border Lie still want the problem to remain containable inside Lordaeron's geography or Quel'Thalas's politics.
pov.chapterTest: The chapter asks whether Sylvanas can say aloud what the whole investigation has been teaching her — that the border is no longer the job because the threat no longer respects the border.
pov.forcedChoice: Return home under authority, or go north as a deserter toward the source.
pov.endStateShift: Sylvanas stops treating north as a probable direction and turns it into an operational order.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 20 opens on the northbound coast and the practical problem of crossing with almost nothing left but the partnership.

throughPov.visibleFunction: Analytical counterweight, north-pointing witness, and the woman whose relationship to Dalaran is about to be redefined from the outside in.
throughPov.povPressure: Sylvanas can see Jaina's direction clearly before she hears any spoken choice from her.
throughPov.interactionRule: No interiority. Use notes, posture, the stone's light, and how Jaina falls into formation once the word "north" is spoken.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Bonded through witness, now waiting to see whether that bond can survive being chosen over family, rank, and home.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: The partnership stops being only a field condition and becomes the structure both women choose against their institutions.
team.doNotOverstateRule: Let the parallel severance show the bond. Do not narrate it ahead of the scene.

entries.rule: Camp order, outrider discipline, and the quiet practical lane that lets the principals spend time on map, sister, and stone.
Younger sister first in effect even when she starts in formal register. The recall must arrive through document, title, and family wound together.
Off-page but vivid through Vereesa's report; his deterioration must feel physical.
Present only through the sending-stone and only as body-language read from Sylvanas's angle.
Institutional infrastructure at its last usable edge.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 20 opens on the coast and the voyage north, with institutional scaffolding stripped away and the partnership now carrying the whole pursuit.

scenes.title: The Camp
scenes.sceneFunction: Reframe south as temporary aftermath ground and north as the next real direction before any institution arrives to contest it.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Jaina's revised source model points in the same direction as the prince.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep camp, smoke, horses, map case, and road direction active so the choice feels like something bodies and supplies have to carry.

enrichment.detail: Thalassian field horses should read as fine-boned endurance patrol mounts with quiet tack, steady even around magic; that gives the camp's order and the later northbound turn a specifically Farstrider texture rather than generic cavalry handling.

beats.goal: Assess the formation's state one day after Stratholme.
beats.conflict: The camp is orderly, but nothing about the order changes what still burns on the northern horizon or what that burn means for every next choice.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas gets a clean read on bodies, horses, and readiness, but the camp itself confirms that south was only an exit lane, not a plan.

newOnPageInformation: Stratholme still burns heavily enough to stain the sky from camp.
Jaina is back to working with notes in her hands rather than standing stunned, which matters more than visible comfort would.
Cyndia has already re-established a functioning Farstrider camp with minimal resources.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the chapter ends with these women going north or being turned back by authority.
How much time the coast road still gives them before Arthas gains too much sea lead.
sensoryAnchors: brown-black smoke on the northern horizon
small campfire and horses picketed tight
Jaina emerging with papers before she reaches food or conversation
beats.transitionIntent: causal-pressure: once the bodies and smoke are set, the map has to make the choice visible.

beats.goal: Lay the accumulated evidence down and test whether it still points in more than one direction.
beats.conflict: The old grain trail still exists on paper, but every meaningful line now bends north: Arthas, the source model, the resonance thread, and the vanishing utility of more southbound proof.

outcome.summary: The direction clarifies, but clarity strips away every remaining excuse to stay inside institutional cover.

beats.valueShift: direction problem -> north becomes the real answer
newOnPageInformation: Further village work south would refine known facts, not change the core picture.
Jaina's frequency model and Farstrider road intelligence converge independently on the same vector.
Northrend is no longer a theory lane; it is the next operational target.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Who will force the choice into spoken form first.
What outside cost arrives before they can turn the map into motion.
sensoryAnchors: map case stretched over supply crates
ranging pin marking camp south of the city
red contamination overlays crossing trader roads toward northern ports
beats.transitionIntent: question-pressure: the chapter has named north, so something from home now has to arrive and demand the opposite.

scenes.title: The Sister
scenes.sceneFunction: Bring the Convocation into camp in the most personal form possible and force Sylvanas to break the Lie aloud.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: North is the right answer on the map.
The institutions have not yet said their piece.
scenes.arcPressure: The force here lives in title, document, rank, family language, and the fact that other people in camp can hear it.

enrichment.detail: Vereesa is canonically the youngest Windrunner sister and historically the one who admired and emulated Sylvanas most closely; sending her instead of a clerk weaponizes family memory and Farstrider identity at the same time.

beats.goal: Identify the incoming riders and read the shape of the problem before the lead rider even dismounts.
beats.conflict: Cyndia's intercept reads clean, which means this is not enemy trouble. It is home trouble, and the lead rider's posture says family before the face confirms it.

outcome.summary: The threat is not military, but that only makes it harder to brush off.

beats.valueShift: north becomes the real answer -> home arrives to contest it
newOnPageInformation: The Convocation sent Vereesa, not a magistrix or clerk.
Vereesa rode hard enough to spend arrows and horse strength on the road south.
Sylvanas recognizes at once that the messenger choice is itself the tactic.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Vereesa is only carrying orders or also carrying real field intelligence from Silvermoon.
How private this conversation can remain in a working camp.
sensoryAnchors: bird-call recognition signals at the camp edge
gray mare lathered through the chest
ink on a ranger's hands that should have been occupied by a bow
beats.transitionIntent: causal-pressure: once family is in camp, the document has to appear and turn emotion into institution.

beats.conflict: Vereesa has to speak as Ranger Captain first while standing in front of her sister, and the document is designed to make public refusal legible as desertion.

outcome.summary: The order lands with full authority, and it is worse than a request because it explicitly threatens exile through military law.

beats.valueShift: home arrives to contest it -> institution becomes personal weapon
newOnPageInformation: The Convocation suspended Sylvanas's foreign deployment authority immediately.
Noncompliance will be treated as desertion, not ordinary disobedience.
The isolationist faction used Stratholme itself as evidence that this was never their war.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How far Vereesa personally agrees with what she just delivered.
Whether Sylvanas will answer as sister, general, or something harsher.
sensoryAnchors: red wax on good vellum
formal register cracking only after the last word
untouched document hanging between the sisters' hands
beats.transitionIntent: escalation: after the order comes the cost report — the one person in Silvermoon who kept fighting for this mission.

beats.emotion: Guilt carried through someone else's exhaustion.
beats.dilemma: Treat Lor'themar's report as useful political intelligence, or let Vereesa's description turn it back into the price this mission has been charging at home.
beats.valueShift: institution becomes personal weapon -> political cost turns concrete
newOnPageInformation: Lor'themar spent nearly everything he had left securing a tiny forward mobilization.
The wit and polish from earlier chapters are gone; he is down to numbers and bone.
Even that small win may not survive the next chamber session.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the crack in Silvermoon's wall widens in time to matter.
What Cyndia makes of hearing her lord hollowed out on Sylvanas's account.
sensoryAnchors: Vereesa's ink-stained fingers describing chamber records and vote counts
Lor'themar reduced to names, dates, and contamination totals in a flat voice
Cyndia listening from the camp edge without comment
beats.transitionIntent: choice-pressure: the only answer left is the one that breaks the Lie in the open.

beats.goal: Answer the recall and settle the question of whether the border still defines the duty.
beats.conflict: Vereesa drops the formal lane and argues family — Alleria gone, the Windrunner name at stake, home still reachable if Sylvanas turns back now. Sylvanas cannot answer with comfort because the evidence has already moved past comfort.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas refuses and says aloud the truth the investigation built in her: the threat to Quel'Thalas is wherever Arthas goes next.

beats.valueShift: political cost turns concrete -> the Lie breaks aloud
newOnPageInformation: Vereesa's real argument is family continuity, not chamber protocol.
Sylvanas now states explicitly that the border no longer contains the job.
Everyone in earshot hears the refusal, which means the desertion is no longer theoretical.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Vereesa can carry the refusal home without breaking from it.
Whether Jaina will mirror this choice or only understand it.
sensoryAnchors: childhood name surfacing once and landing harder than rank ever could
untouched recall still in Vereesa's hands
camp quiet enough for the refusal to travel farther than either sister wanted
beats.transitionIntent: mirror-pull: Sylvanas has broken from one institution, so the chapter now needs the quieter severance on the other side of camp.

scenes.title: The Parallel Severance
scenes.sceneFunction: Mirror Sylvanas's public refusal with Jaina's observed, quieter break from Dalaran, then send Vereesa home carrying the cost.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Sylvanas has already chosen north in substance if not yet as road order.
scenes.arcPressure: Jaina's stone scene must stay readable from outside only. The stone, the light, and the posture do the interpretive work.

enrichment.detail: Antonidas is not just Jaina's mentor but the head of the Kirin Tor and de facto ruler of Dalaran, so a single observed exchange on the stone carries both paternal and institutional weight even when Sylvanas never hears the words.

beats.goal: Observe Jaina's answer to Dalaran without trespassing into her interior.
beats.conflict: Sylvanas cannot hear the words, only the body carrying them — and the body says this is notification, not negotiation.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas cannot know the exact language, but she gets the only fact she needs: Jaina is not asking permission to go north.

beats.valueShift: the Lie breaks aloud -> the parallel choice becomes visible
newOnPageInformation: Jaina initiated the second call; Antonidas did not force this one on her.
The stone goes dark when it ends, unlike the lingering warmth from the unresolved check-in the night before.
Jaina stores the stone like a tool she no longer expects to use soon.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What Antonidas said back.
What that severance cost Jaina privately.
sensoryAnchors: amber pulse of the stone at the camp edge
straight spine and set jaw during the call
dark crystal once the connection dies
beats.transitionIntent: causal-close: once Jaina's institution is cut, the chapter can let the sister ride home with the other refusal.

beats.goal: Close the family scene and make the cost of north visible in a body riding away.
beats.conflict: There is no version of this goodbye that heals anything. The best they can do is make it clean enough to survive.

outcome.summary: The departure happens, but it leaves the camp smaller and the north road more expensive than it was an hour earlier.

beats.valueShift: the parallel choice becomes visible -> family cost becomes absence
newOnPageInformation: Vereesa accepts the refusal even while hating what it means.
The recall returns to Silvermoon unanswered except by Sylvanas's absence.
Cyndia witnesses both Windrunner departures without intruding on either.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the sisters will see each other again.
What story the Convocation tells once Vereesa rides back without the Ranger-General.
sensoryAnchors: recall document folded into Vereesa's saddlebag
gray mare turning north under a rider who wanted the opposite answer
dust hanging after the escort is gone
beats.transitionIntent: launch-pressure: with both institutions answered, nothing remains between the formation and the road order except saying it out loud.

scenes.title: The Road North
scenes.sceneFunction: Convert agreement into movement and make clear that the relay network, the map, and Jaina's senses all point the same way.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Both women have now chosen the mission over home.
North only lacks the spoken order.
scenes.arcPressure: Travel texture again — this scene needs coast-road practicality, horses, sea air, dwindling infrastructure, and the bodily fact of turning into colder country.

enrichment.detail: Northern Lordaeron should thin from prosperous interior roads into harsher coastal approach country; by the time they near the harbors, the salt arrives before the water and the relay network is already at its last practical edge. Northrend's cold also presses south conceptually here, letting the route feel like a real threshold rather than a generic ride.
enrichment.source: stories/the-plague-road/locations/lordaeron-and-northrend.md; Warcraft Wiki: https://warcraft.wiki.gg/wiki/Lordaeron; Warcraft Wiki: https://warcraft.wiki.gg/wiki/Northrend

beats.goal: Speak the direction and set the formation moving.
beats.conflict: The order is operationally obvious and personally irreversible. Once spoken, it turns analysis and refusal into desertion in motion.

outcome.summary: The order is given and the formation answers it at once, but every institutional support they relied on is now behind them.

beats.valueShift: family cost becomes absence -> pursuit becomes chosen fact
newOnPageInformation: Sylvanas gives the order in a single word.
Jaina falls into formation without question, confirming convergence rather than debate.
North is now a shared decision rather than a map inference.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How they secure passage once the land runs out.
How far behind Arthas they already are in practical terms.
sensoryAnchors: tents struck and loads redistributed fast
horses turned and moving before the dust from Vereesa's departure fully settles
Jaina already in her left-rear lane when the order finishes sounding
beats.transitionIntent: forward-pull: once north is spoken, the road itself has to start teaching what north now costs.

beats.goal: Establish what north travel means materially before the sea crossing begins.
beats.conflict: The relay network thins toward the coast, the air changes, and Jaina's face says the frequency is getting stronger even though Sylvanas cannot feel it herself.

outcome.summary: They have a workable next route and real confirmation that the source pull strengthens northward, but everything beyond the coast will have to run on borrowed passage and the partnership alone.

beats.valueShift: pursuit becomes chosen fact -> investigation fully becomes pursuit
newOnPageInformation: The last useful relay stations end at the northern harbors.
Sea air and colder wind arrive before the water itself does.
Jaina's resonance sense reacts more strongly as they ride into the northward pull.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Which ship they get.
What waits in Northrend besides Arthas and the source.
sensoryAnchors: salt wind reaching them before the coast is visible
horses breathing harder in colder evening air
Jaina's expression changing as if she has found a track the others cannot see
beats.transitionIntent: handoff-pressure: Chapter 20 opens on that coast-road pressure and the practical work of getting across the water.

factsLockedForNextChapter: Sylvanas refused the Convocation and is now a deserter in practice.
Jaina severed the Dalaran line herself.
Lor'themar bought only a small forward mobilization and paid heavily for it.
The relay network ends at the coast.
Jaina's resonance model grows stronger northward.
The formation is riding for the northern harbors and a ship to Northrend.
factsNotYetEarned: Any ship captain or vessel choice.
Any easing of the resource deficit they are riding into.

warnings: Beats 1-4 run four consecutive scene beats before the lone sequel at beat 5; keep the opening pressure modes distinct so the camp-to-recall run does not flatten.
Beats 6-10 return to a five-beat scene run after beat 5, so the road-side half needs breathing room inside the draft rather than extra explanatory pauses.
Scene outcomes mix yes-but and no-and effectively, but there is no clean yes in the chapter; every compromised success will need its own cost texture in prose.
Normalized hybrid Scene/Sequel labels at beats 6, 7, and 10 to scene beats because the outline supplies goal/conflict/outcome structure rather than sequel fields.
